title: Centella arbuscula
taxonomy: Centella arbuscula belongs to the family Apiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species in the Apiaceae family. Apiaceae is a family of flowering plants that includes a wide range of species, from herbs and vegetables to wildflowers and shrubs. The family is primarily composed of plants with umbels, which are clusters of flowers arranged in a spherical or umbrella-like shape. Within the Apiaceae family, Centella arbuscula is often classified as a member of the tribe Scandiceae.

intro: Centella arbuscula, also known as European toothwort, is a perennial plant of the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its distinctive toothed leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to improve cognitive function, reduce anxiety and stress, and support skin health.
